title: Stock Market ~Buying a Part of a Company~
description: Let's learn about the stock market with Gaku-chan in a fun and easy way! 📊

scenes.speech: What is a stock? Let's explore together! 📊
scenes.title: Stock Market

scenes.speech: What does it mean to buy stocks?
scenes.title: What are Stocks?
scenes.content: Companies issue stocks to raise money for their businesses. When you buy one stock, you become a part-owner of that company! If the company grows, the value of your stock will increase.
scenes.highlight: Shareholders = owners of the company. You also receive dividends (a share of the profits)!

scenes.speech: What is the Tokyo Stock Exchange?
scenes.title: How the Stock Exchange Works
scenes.content: The Tokyo Stock Exchange (TSE) is a market where stocks are bought and sold. Currently, there are three markets: Prime, Standard, and Growth. Famous stock exchanges around the world include the NYSE (New York) and NASDAQ.
scenes.highlight: TSE Prime: Approximately 1,800 companies are listed, with a total market capitalization of over 800 trillion yen (as of 2024)

scenes.speech: Why do stock prices go up and down?
scenes.title: Why Stock Prices Move
scenes.content: Stock prices are determined by the balance between demand (people who want to buy) and supply (people who want to sell). If a company's performance is good, more people will want to buy, and the stock price will rise. News, economic indicators, and global events also have a big impact.
scenes.highlight: Stock price = a numerical representation of the 'expectations' for that company

scenes.speech: What are the Nikkei Average and TOPIX?
scenes.title: Understanding Stock Indices
scenes.content: Nikkei Average: the average stock price of 225 representative Japanese companies. TOPIX: an index that shows the overall movement of the TSE. S&P 500: an index of 500 US companies. Dow: an index of 30 US companies. These are like economic thermometers!
scenes.highlight: Nikkei Average: around 40,000 yen (a record high in March 2024) → the first time since the bubble era!

scenes.speech: What happens when you own stocks in famous companies?
scenes.title: Shareholder Rights
scenes.content: As a shareholder, you have the rights to: ① dividends (a share of the company's profits), ② shareholder benefits (such as product discounts or coupons), and ③ voting rights (you can vote on important company decisions). Toyota shareholders even get discounts on Toyota cars!
scenes.highlight: Examples of shareholder benefits: McDonald's stock → free burger coupons, Nintendo → game discounts

scenes.speech: I'll teach you tips for not losing money on stocks!
scenes.title: Basic Stock Investment Strategies
scenes.content: ① Buy & Hold (buy and hold for a long time), ② diversify your investments (hold multiple stocks), and ③ dollar-cost averaging (invest a fixed amount of money at regular intervals). Try to make time work for you, rather than trying to time the market!
scenes.highlight: Dollar-cost averaging: you can buy more stocks when prices are low and fewer when prices are high → average cost decreases

scenes.speech: Let's research stocks in companies you're interested in!
scenes.title: Try Virtual Investment!
scenes.content: Check the stock prices of your favorite brands (Apple, Toyota, Nintendo, etc.). Compare the price a year ago to the current price to see how much it has fluctuated. You can check stock prices on apps or brokerage websites!
scenes.highlight: You can check stock prices daily on free apps: Yahoo! Finance, SBI Securities, etc.

scenes.speech: Unlisted stocks (IPOs) are like lottery tickets!?
scenes.title: What is an IPO (Initial Public Offering)?
scenes.content: An IPO is when a company lists its stocks on the stock market for the first time. Popular IPO stocks can increase in value several times on the first day of listing! But there's also a risk of losing money. It's like a lottery, and you need to apply to buy the stocks.
scenes.highlight: Famous IPO examples: LINE (2016), Mercari (2018), Rare Job → listing price 2-5 times higher

scenes.speech: You've learned about the stock market! Next, let's learn about the magic of compound interest! 🎉
scenes.title: Lesson Completed!
